title: Fix test flakes caused by improper podman-logs
body:
This one has been a thorn in my side: it's a podman-log issue, but not remote, so I _almost_ retitled #16132 (removing "remote").

Nope, it's a bug in the tests themselves. One solution would be to podman-wait, but I see no reason for logs to be involved, so I went with podman start -a instead. This removes the k8s-log stuff which is no longer necessary. Cleanup all around.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

```release-note
None
```

/hold cancel

I think this serves as a belated confirmation of #16401 : tests were skipped, but Windows didn't fail.
